meta.title: Dawood Saleh | Software Engineer
meta.description: Software engineer building serverless backends, AI tooling, and infrastructure that runs without maintenance.

hero.subtitle: Software engineer building serverless backends, AI tooling, and infrastructure that runs without maintenance.

writing.heading: From the notebook
writing.subheading: Essays, walkthroughs, and notes from the field.
writing.author: Author

about.bio1: I got into programming because I liked understanding how things work — that's still what drives most of what I build. Over the past few years I've been working independently, building backend systems, Telegram bots, and tools that connect AI models to real data.
about.bio2: One project I kept coming back to was MCP — a protocol that lets AI models use external tools and live data sources. I built an MCP server for Arabic academic research because the gap was obvious: millions of Arabic research records with almost no AI-accessible tooling around them.
about.bio3: Before going independent I taught OOP at Hajjah University for about seven months. Teaching is useful that way — you can't hide behind vague code when someone's waiting for an explanation.

items.title: Arabic Academic Research MCP Server
items.description: MCP server integrating 4M+ records from Mandumah, ASJP, and SHAMAA — the first AI-accessible tooling for Arabic academic research. Published as an open tool for the Arabic research community.

items.title: Multi-Platform Telegram Media Bot
items.description: Telegram media downloader supporting 9+ platforms, deployed on Cloudflare Workers. Built with Node.js and Hono, featuring analytics tracking and cron-based automation.

items.title: Serverless Backend Systems
items.description: Architected serverless backends on Cloudflare Workers with KV storage, webhook automation, and scheduled tasks. Designed to be API-driven and maintenance-free.

contact.description: Open to backend engineering roles and serious freelance work. If the project involves AI tooling or serverless, that's my territory.
